title: ajuga pyramidalis subsp. meonantha
intro: Ajuga pyramidalis subsp. meonantha, also known as Bugle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Ajuga pyramidalis subsp. meonantha include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant often include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
